Though LPG is an extremely good domestic fuel because it has high calorific value, and burns smokeless but it can be dangerous too. LPG is a highly inflammable gas, that is, it catches fire easily. Any leakage of LPG from the gas cylinder, stove or the rubber pipe (connecting the cylinder and stove) will form an explosive mixture with air in the kitchen. And on lighting the matchstick an explosion will take place, the whole kitchen will be set on fire and the person working in the kitchen may get burnt. In fact, many such accidents occur quite often and should be avoided by observing a few precautions.
1. L.P.G. is a good domestic fuel because
(a) high calorific value
(b) highly inflamable
(c) burn with smoke
(d) low calorific value
2. Though L.P.G. does not produce poisonous gas on burning but it is dangerous too, because
(a) L.P.G. is highly inflamable and catches fire easily on leakage
(b) It burns with smokeless flame
(c) It is easy to handle and convenient to store
(d) It's calorific value is about 50 k J / g

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q1
rows.question: What is a good source of energy ?
rows.answer:
A good source of energy has the following characteristics:
(i) It could do a large amount of work per unit volume of mass.
(ii) It should be easily accessible.
(iii) It should be easy to store and transport.
(iv) It should be economical.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q2
rows.question: What is a good fuel ?
rows.answer:
(a) It should have high calorific value.
(b) It should have low ignition temperature.
(c) It should not leave more ashes.
(d) It should not produce harmful gases on burning.
(e) It should be economic and easy to handle.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q3
rows.question: If you could use any source of energy for heating your food, which one would you use and why?
rows.answer:
If we are in a rural area, gobar-gas or biogas should be used as they are easily available there and are almost pollution free. Also, they have high calorific value.
In a city, LPG should be used since it doesn't produce pollution, it is easily available and economical than other sources.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q4
rows.question: What are the disadvantages of fossil fuels ?
rows.answer:
(i) They are formed under the earth's surface by a long process which takes million of years to form.
(ii) It is non-renewable source of energy.
(iii) They cause air pollution

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q5
rows.question: Why are we looking at alternate sources of energy?
rows.answer: Because of our changing life style and new technology we want more efficient fuels which are pollution free, cheap and renewable which cannot be fulfilled by using traditional sources of energy.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q6
rows.question: How has the traditional use of wind \& water energy been modified for our convenience?
rows.answer: Earlier, the wind mills were used to harnesswind energy to do mechanical work such as lifting/drawing water from well. Today, windmills are used to generate electricity. Traditionally, water falls were used as a source of potential energy which was converted into electricity with the help of turbines. Today, hydrodams are used to harness potential energy.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q7
rows.question: What kind of mirror-concave, convex or plane- would be best suited for use in a solar cooker? Why?
rows.answer: Plane mirror is best suited for a solar cooker because it reflects the parallel rays of sunlight to the top of solar cooker box in the form of a strong beam of sunlight, which helps the food to cook fast.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q8
rows.question: What are the limitations of the energy that can be obtained from the oceans?
rows.answer:
The energy from the ocean can be obtained mainly in three forms- (i) tidal energy (ii) wave energy and ocean thermal energy. But these energy can't be a potential source of energy in future because of the following reasons -
(i) there are very few places around the world which are suitable for building tidal dams.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q1
rows.question: Why CNG considered as environmental friendly fuel ?
rows.answer: CNG gas create less pollution.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q2
rows.question: Name two main combustible components of biogas.
rows.answer:
(i) Methane
(ii) Hydrogen.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q3
rows.question: Name the major constituent of natural gas.
rows.answer: The major constituent of natural gas is methane.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q4
rows.question: State the energy transformation taking place when a boy is ridding a bicycle.
rows.answer: Muscular energy to Mechanical energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q5
rows.question: Name the main constituents of bio-gas.
rows.answer: Methane is the main constituent of Biogas.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q6
rows.question: In what respect fuel oil is better than coal ?
rows.answer:
(i) Fuel oil has higher calorific value than coal.
(ii) Fuel oil does not leave ash or residue.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q7
rows.question: How has the traditional use of wind and water energy been modified for our convenience?
rows.answer: The traditional use of wind has been modified by using windmills and that of water by constructing hydroelectric power plants.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q8
rows.question: What is geothermal energy?
rows.answer: The energy extracted from hot water springs on the earth is called geothermal energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q9
rows.question: Name two energy sources that you would consider to be renewable. Give reasons for your choices.
rows.answer:
(i) wind energy
(ii) solar energy
These sources of energy can be used again and again endlessly. They will never get exhausted.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q10
rows.question: Give the names of two energy sources that you would consider to be exhaustible. Give reasons for your choices.
rows.answer:
(i) Fossil fuels
(ii) Nuclear fuels
Fossil fuels are present in a limited amount in the earth. Once exhausted, they will not be available to us again. The nuclear material which can be conveniently extracted from the earth are limited and hence they will get exhausted one day.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q11
rows.question: What is a turbine?
rows.answer: It is a machine for converting the kinetic energy of the fluid (flowing water or gas) into mechanical energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q12
rows.question: What do you mean by hydro energy?
rows.answer: The energy of water is called hydro energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q13
rows.question: What do you mean by tidal energy?
rows.answer: The enormous movement of water between the high tides and low tides provides a source of energy, which is called tidal energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q21
rows.question: Explain generation of electricity in a thermal power plant.
rows.answer: Large amount of fossil fuels are burnt everyday in power stations to heat up water to produce steam which further runs the turbine to generate electricity. Many thermal plants are set up near coal or oil fields. In these plants fuel is burnt to produce heat energy which is converted into electrical energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q22
rows.question: If energy can neither be created nor destroyed, explain with an example why we should worry about our energy resources?
rows.answer:
The law of conservation of energy states that energy can neither be created nor be destroyed i.e. total energy remains constant. But, it can only be converted from one from to another. Despite this fact, the world is facing energy crisis. The reason behind this is that energy is converted into non-usable forms.
Example: Burning of candle → light + heat but products cannot produce chemical energy.
Fossil fuels like coal, petroleum etc. are the sources of energy which have accumulated in nature over a long time and cannot be replaced. We should worry about such sources because these sources of energy are getting depleted and sooner or late, will no longer be available to us.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q23
rows.question: Make a list of three features due to which L.P.G is considered to be a good fuel?
rows.answer:
(i) It releases large amount of energy on burning
(ii) It is easy to store
(iii) It is easy to transport
(iv) It does not produce smoke
(v) Environment friendly

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q24
rows.question: Explain the term geothermal energy'. How can it be expoited? Though it is economical yet it is not harnessed in most of the countries. Why?
rows.answer:
The heat from interior of the earth can be utilised as a source of energy under certain favourable conditions that are created by natural processes. This is known as geothermal energy.
Molten rocks formed in the deeper hot regions of Earth's crust are pushed upward and trapped in certain regions due to geological changes. These spots are called hot spots. When underground water comes in contact with hot spots, steam is generated. Steam trapped in rocks is routed through a pipe to turbine to generate electricity. This steam can be used to run turbines connected to electric generators.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q25
rows.question: List three advantages of harnessing wind energy.
rows.answer:
The advantages of harnessing wind energy are:
(i) It is an environment-friendly and efficient source of renewable energy.
(ii) It requires no recurring expenses for the production of electricity.
(iii) It does not cause any pollution.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q3
rows.question: Electricity generated at hydroelectric power stations is considered to be another form of solar energy. Explain.
rows.answer: The energy of water (or hydro-energy) is in fact an indirect source of solar energy because it is the solar energy which is responsible for water cycle. The heat of solar energy evaporates water from ocean and the surface of the earth. The water vapours rise high in the atmosphere, get cooled and fall back to the earth in the form of rain and snow. The rain water and the water formed by melting of snow then flows rapidly in the rivers and provides us with hydro energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q4
rows.question:
(a) Describe the steps involved in obtaining biogas and explain what is meant by anaerobic decomposition.
(b) Which isotope of uranium can undergo fission readily?
rows.answer:
(a) Anaerobic decomposition : The process in which the complex compound of cow dung slurry decomposes or breaks down in the absence of oxygen by anaerobic micro-organisms called anaerobic bacteria is known as anaerobic decomposition.
It generates gases like methane ( 75 % ), carbon dioxide, hydrogen and hydrogen sulphide.
Steps involved in obtaining biogas :
(i) Slurry is made by mixing of animal dung with an equal amount of water.
(ii) Slurry is passed through an inlet chamber of an underground digester tank.
(iii) In digester tank, slurry is decomposed by anaerobic bacteria in about 50-60 days to produce biogas.
(iv) The biogas collected in domes built over the digester tank and has a gas outlet with value.
(v) The pressure exerted by the biogas on the slurry forces the spent slurry to the overflow tank via outlet chamber.
(vi) The spent slurry is periodically removed and used as a good manure.
(vii) The whole process is repeated again for regular supply of biogas.
(b) _92²³⁵ U can undergo fission readily.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q5
rows.question: Explain what causes the wind to blow in equatorial regions. What is wind energy?
rows.answer: Solar energy is responsible for wind to blow. The intensity of sun-rays is much more stronger near the equator of the earth than in the polar region. Due to more intense heat, the air near the surface of the earth in equatorial regions become quite hot. The hot air being lighter rises upward and cooler air from the polar region of earth starts flowing which causes wind to blow from the high pressure to low pressure region. So wind blows to equatorial regions. The energy possessed by this wind is called wind energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q6
rows.question: What is biogas? How can biogas be obtained? Why is the use of biogas obtained from cow dung advised in preference to burning of cow dung cakes?
rows.answer:
Biogas. The gas produced by the decay of biomass in the presence of water by anaerobic micro-organisms in the absence of oxygen is called biogas. Biogas can be obtained by using fixed dome type biogas plant.
The burning of cow-dung cakes is not advisable because of the following reasons :
(i) It produces a lot of ash as residue.
(ii) It produces a lot of smoke causes air pollution as well as creates health hazards.
(iii) It has low heat efficient fuel.
(iv) It destroys the useful manure.
Whereas during the production of biogas only organic matter is decomposed by anaerobic bacteria and convert into biogas. So use of biogas obtained from cow dung advised in preference gives (i) a clean fuel, free from pollution and (ii) spent slurry can be used as a manure.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q7
rows.question: Name three forms in which energy from ocean is made available for use. What are OTEC power plants ? How do they operate?
rows.answer:
Three forms of oceanic energy are
(a) Sea wave energy
(b) Tidal energy
(c) Ocean thermal energy.
OTEC power plant. The plants which are used to harness ocean thermal energy is called OTEC power plant.
Working of OTEC power plant. A temperature difference between warm surface water heated by sun and colder water at deeper level upto 1000 m is 20° C or more is required to operate OTEC plant.
In the OTEC system, the warm surface water is used to boil a liquid like ammonia. The vapour of liquid is then used to rotate the turbine of a generator. The cold water from the deeper level is used to convert the ammonia vapour again into liquid.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q8
rows.question: Describe the construction of solar cooker. How does it cause rise in temperature to cook food?
rows.answer:
Construction. It consists of an insulated metal box which is painted all black from inside. There is thick glass sheet as a cover over the box and a plane mirror reflector attached to the box.
Cooking of food. The food to be cooked is placed in a metal container kept in the box and covered with glass sheet. When solar energy falls on reflector, the reflector sends them to the top of solar cooker in the form of strong beam of sunlight which is absorbed by the black surface in the box. The infra-red rays cause heating effect which raises the temperature to 100° C − 140° C which cooks the food.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q9
rows.question:
(i) Distinguish between renewable and non-renewable sources of energy giving one example of each.
(ii) Why is the use of wood as a fuel not advised although forests can be replenished?
rows.answer:
(i) Renewable sources of energy are those that can be used without depleting their reserves and do not get exhausted e.g., solar energy, wind energy, hydro energy, etc.
Non-renewable sources of energy are those which cannot be replenished and exhausted with the passage of time. e.g., : coal, pertroleum and natural gas.
(ii) Wood is not advisable used as a fuel, although forests can be replenished, due to the following reasons :
(a) Burning of wood causes pollution.
(b) The left-over residue after combustion have a disposed off problem.
(c) Smoke produced by burning of wood causes health hazard.
(d) Wood is a less heat generating fuel.
(e) Trees are essential for our life as they absorb C O2 and give out O2.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Reasoning Based Questions — Q1
rows.question: Coal is said to be formed from the wood of trees. Why then is coal considered to be a non-renewable source of energy whereas wood is a renewable source of energy ?
rows.answer:
Wood is a renewable source of energy because if trees are cut from the forests for obtaining wood, then more trees will grow in the forest in due course of time.
Coal is a non-renewable source of energy because coal has accumulated in the earth over a very, very long time and if all the coal gets exhausted, it cannot be produced quickly in nature.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Reasoning Based Questions — Q2
rows.question: The energy in petrol originally came from the sun. Explain how it get into petrol.
rows.answer: Petrol is a fossi fuel which is produced from the remains of plants and animals that were buried in the earth long ago. The green plants need sunlight energy to grow. They get this energy from sunlight and store in the form of carbon compounds. The animals also eat plants, so the animal material is also a store house of sun's energy thus we can say, the energy in pertol originally came from the sun.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Reasoning Based Questions — Q3
rows.question: Why is the copper tube of a solar water heater painted black from outside?
rows.answer: Because black painted surface absorbs more heat rays of the sun.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Reasoning Based Questions — Q4
rows.question: Which of the two is a cleaner fuel : Hydrogen or CNG ? Why?
rows.answer: Hydrogen is a cleaner fuel than CNG. This is because burning of hydrogen produces only water which is not harmful. On the other hand, burning of CNG produces carbon dioxide gas and water. This carbon dioxide can produce greenhouse effect in the atmosphere and lead to the excessive heating of the environment.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q2
rows.question: Water that falls from a waterfall is used to generate electricity. If the height of water fall is 100 m and 10² m³ of water falls every minute, then find the hydel energy in MW h generated in one day. Assume that 60 % of the energy of the flowing water is converted into electricity. (Take density of water as 1000 k g m−³ ).
rows.answer:
Let the volume of water that flows in every minute = V.
Density of water (d) = m/V
One day = 24 × 60 minutes
Mass of the water that flows in one day is m= V d × (60 × 24)
Potential energy, PE = m g h
The energy of the flowing water in one day is = m g h 60 % of the ( mgh ) is used to generate electricity
∴ Energy generated = 24 M W h

rows.answer: C H4 is a better fuel.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q4
rows.question: Find the electrical power generated in 1 s by a wind mill that is erected in a location when air flows with an average speed of 54 k m h−¹. Assume that the moving air is completely stopped by the blades of the wind turbine of area 10 m², over which air flows normally and the energy conversion from wind energy to electrical energy is only 50 %. (Take density of air = 1.3 k g m−³ )
rows.answer:
The volume of air that makes blades to rotate = V= ℓ × area of the blade
Velocity (v) = ℓ/t
Mass of the air = m= volume × density of air
Kinetic energy (KE) of air = 1/2 m v²
50 % of kinetic energy of air is converted into electrical energy (EE)
The power of electrical generation is, P= (E E)/(1 s) = 2.53 k W

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q7
rows.question:
Explain why :
(i) It is difficult to burn a piece of wood fresh from a tree.
(ii) Pouring dry sand over the fire extinguishes it.
(iii) It is difficult to use hydrogen as a source of energy.
(iv) Charcoal is considered a better fuel than wood.
rows.answer:
(i) It is because a piece of fresh wood is not dry and therefore it is to be heated at high temperature before it catches fire, that is why it is difficult to burn.
(ii) It cuts off the supply of air (oxygen) which is required for combustion to take place.
(iii) Hydrogen is highly combustible and burns with an explosion, therefore, it is difficult to store and transport.
(iv) Charcoal, has higher calorific value than wood and produces less smoke than wood.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q8
rows.question: Name the major fuel component of biogas. What are its other combustible components? What is the use for the residual slurry and why?
rows.answer:
Major fuel component of biogas-Methane. Other combustile component of biogas-Hydrogen, Hydrogen sulphide, Carbon dioxide.
Use of residue slurry. Left-over slurry from the biogas plant is used as a manure because it is rich in nitrogen and phosphorus compounds and can be used in place of nitrogeneous and phosphorus fertilizers.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q9
rows.question: What is the main basic cause for wind to blow ? Name a part of India where wind energy is commercially harnessed. Compare wind power and power of water flow in respect of generating mechanical and electrical energies. What is the hindrance in developing them ?
rows.answer:
Cause of wind to blow.
Unequal heating of the different land mass and water bodies such as river, lake, ocean etc. by solar radiations creates a different pressure regions. The flow of air from higher pressure region i.e., polar region towards the lower pressure region i.e., equatorial region, constitutes wind.
It is Kanya Kumari in Tamil Nadu where wind energy is commercially harnessed.
Wind power potential. From the wind power India generates only 2000 MW whereas India has the potential to generate 45000 MW of electrical power from wind energy.
Hydro power potential. It is estimated that India can generate about 4 × 10¹¹ k W h of electricity when its hydro power potential is fully exploited. At present, India can exploit only 11 % of its potential capacity.

= 200 M e V= 200 × 1.6 × 10−¹³ J= 3.2 × 10−¹¹ J
Number of fission per second required

rows.title: ILLUSTRATION : 5.2
rows.question:
Whcih of the following is the fusion reaction
(a) 1 H² + 1 H² → 2 H e⁴
(b) 0 n¹ + 7 N¹⁴ → 6 C¹⁴ + 1 H¹
(c) 0 n¹ + 92 U²³⁸ → 93 N p²³⁹ + β−¹ + γ
(d) 1 H³ → 2 H e³ + β−¹ + γ
rows.answer:
(a) 1 H² + 1 H² → 2 H e⁴
In nuclear fusion reaction, two or more that two lighter nuelei combine to form a single heavy nucleus.
## MISCELLANEOUS SOLVED EXAMPLES
1. In afission reaction _92²³⁶ U → ¹¹⁷ X + ¹¹⁷ Y + n + n, the binding energy per nucleon of X and Y is 8.5 MeV whereas of ²³⁶ U is 7.6 MeV . The total energy liberated will be about
(a) 200 KeV
(b) 2 KeV
(c) 2 0 0 M e V
(d) 2000 MeV
Sol. Here, total energy released
Δ E= × (217 + 217) − y (236)
= 8.5 × 234 − 7.6 × 236
= 195.4 M e V
2. The binding enegy of deutron _1² H is 1. 1 1 2 M e V per nucleon and an a-particle _2⁴ H He hasa binding energy of 7.047 MeV per nucleon. Then in the fusion reaction _1² H + _1² H → _2⁴ H e + Q, the energy Q released is.
Sol. Energy equivalent to = 2 × 1.112= 2.224 M e V
Energyequivalento 2 H e⁴= 4 × 7.047= 28.188 M e V
From the equation,energy released = 28.188 − 2 × 2.224= 23.74 M e V ≈ 24 M e V
3. The binding energy/nucleon of deuteron (𝐇²) and the helium atom (H e⁴) are 1. 1 M e V and 7 M e V respectively. If the two deuteron atoms fuse to form a single helium atom, then, How much energy is released ?
Sol. Binding energy of deuteron (1 H²)
= 2 × 1.1 M e V= 2.2 M e V
Binding energy of helium atom ( 2 H e⁴ )
= 4 × 7 M e V= 28 M e V
1 H² + 1 H² → 2 H e⁴ + Energy released
Energy released = B E of helium − 2 × B E of deuteron
= 28 M e V − 2 × 2.2 M e V= 23.6 M e V
4. What is the power output of a 9 2 U²³⁵ reactor if it takes 3 0 days to use up 2 k g of fuel, and if each fission gives 1 8 5 MeV of usable energy?
Sol. The number of 9 2 U²³⁵ in 2 kg ,
N & = ((2 × 1000))/(235) × 6.02 × 10²³ & = 5.12 × 10²⁴
The fission of each 92 U²³⁵ produces energy
= 185 M e V
∴ Total energy produces,
E & = 5.12 × 10²⁴ × (185 × 1.6 × 10⁻¹³) J & = 1515.52 × 10¹¹ J
The power output P= E/t= (1515.52 × 10¹¹)/(30 × 24 × 3600) = 5.84 × 10⁷ watt
= 6.06 × 10⁷ W

## Passage Based Questions

## Passage
Though LPG is an extremely good domestic fuel because it has high calorific value, and burns smokeless but it can be dangerous too. LPG is a highly inflammable gas, that is, it catches fire easily. Any leakage of LPG from the gas cylinder, stove or the rubber pipe (connecting the cylinder and stove) will form an explosive mixture with air in the kitchen. And on lighting the matchstick an explosion will take place, the whole kitchen will be set on fire and the person working in the kitchen may get burnt. In fact, many such accidents occur quite often and should be avoided by observing a few precautions.
1. L.P.G. is a good domestic fuel because
(a) high calorific value
(b) highly inflamable
(c) burn with smoke
(d) low calorific value
2. Though L.P.G. does not produce poisonous gas on burning but it is dangerous too, because
(a) L.P.G. is highly inflamable and catches fire easily on leakage
(b) It burns with smokeless flame
(c) It is easy to handle and convenient to store
(d) It's calorific value is about 50 k J / g

rows.answer: The traditional use of wind has been modified by using windmills and that of water by constructing hydroelectric power plants.

rows.answer: The energy extracted from hot water springs on the earth is called geothermal energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q9
rows.question: Name two energy sources that you would consider to be renewable. Give reasons for your choices.
rows.answer:
(i) wind energy
(ii) solar energy
These sources of energy can be used again and again endlessly. They will never get exhausted.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Very Short Answer Questions — Q10
rows.question: Give the names of two energy sources that you would consider to be exhaustible. Give reasons for your choices.
rows.answer:
(i) Fossil fuels
(ii) Nuclear fuels
Fossil fuels are present in a limited amount in the earth. Once exhausted, they will not be available to us again. The nuclear material which can be conveniently extracted from the earth are limited and hence they will get exhausted one day.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q21
rows.question: Explain generation of electricity in a thermal power plant.
rows.answer: Large amount of fossil fuels are burnt everyday in power stations to heat up water to produce steam which further runs the turbine to generate electricity. Many thermal plants are set up near coal or oil fields. In these plants fuel is burnt to produce heat energy which is converted into electrical energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q22
rows.question: If energy can neither be created nor destroyed, explain with an example why we should worry about our energy resources?
rows.answer:
The law of conservation of energy states that energy can neither be created nor be destroyed i.e. total energy remains constant. But, it can only be converted from one from to another. Despite this fact, the world is facing energy crisis. The reason behind this is that energy is converted into non-usable forms.
Example: Burning of candle → light + heat but products cannot produce chemical energy.
Fossil fuels like coal, petroleum etc. are the sources of energy which have accumulated in nature over a long time and cannot be replaced. We should worry about such sources because these sources of energy are getting depleted and sooner or late, will no longer be available to us.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q23
rows.question: Make a list of three features due to which L.P.G is considered to be a good fuel?
rows.answer:
(i) It releases large amount of energy on burning
(ii) It is easy to store
(iii) It is easy to transport
(iv) It does not produce smoke
(v) Environment friendly

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q24
rows.question: Explain the term geothermal energy'. How can it be expoited? Though it is economical yet it is not harnessed in most of the countries. Why?
rows.answer:
The heat from interior of the earth can be utilised as a source of energy under certain favourable conditions that are created by natural processes. This is known as geothermal energy.
Molten rocks formed in the deeper hot regions of Earth's crust are pushed upward and trapped in certain regions due to geological changes. These spots are called hot spots. When underground water comes in contact with hot spots, steam is generated. Steam trapped in rocks is routed through a pipe to turbine to generate electricity. This steam can be used to run turbines connected to electric generators.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Short Answer Questions — Q25
rows.question: List three advantages of harnessing wind energy.
rows.answer:
The advantages of harnessing wind energy are:
(i) It is an environment-friendly and efficient source of renewable energy.
(ii) It requires no recurring expenses for the production of electricity.
(iii) It does not cause any pollution.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q3
rows.question: Electricity generated at hydroelectric power stations is considered to be another form of solar energy. Explain.
rows.answer: The energy of water (or hydro-energy) is in fact an indirect source of solar energy because it is the solar energy which is responsible for water cycle. The heat of solar energy evaporates water from ocean and the surface of the earth. The water vapours rise high in the atmosphere, get cooled and fall back to the earth in the form of rain and snow. The rain water and the water formed by melting of snow then flows rapidly in the rivers and provides us with hydro energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q4
rows.question:
(a) Describe the steps involved in obtaining biogas and explain what is meant by anaerobic decomposition.
(b) Which isotope of uranium can undergo fission readily?
rows.answer:
(a) Anaerobic decomposition : The process in which the complex compound of cow dung slurry decomposes or breaks down in the absence of oxygen by anaerobic micro-organisms called anaerobic bacteria is known as anaerobic decomposition.
It generates gases like methane ( 75 % ), carbon dioxide, hydrogen and hydrogen sulphide.
Steps involved in obtaining biogas :
(i) Slurry is made by mixing of animal dung with an equal amount of water.
(ii) Slurry is passed through an inlet chamber of an underground digester tank.
(iii) In digester tank, slurry is decomposed by anaerobic bacteria in about 50-60 days to produce biogas.
(iv) The biogas collected in domes built over the digester tank and has a gas outlet with value.
(v) The pressure exerted by the biogas on the slurry forces the spent slurry to the overflow tank via outlet chamber.
(vi) The spent slurry is periodically removed and used as a good manure.
(vii) The whole process is repeated again for regular supply of biogas.
(b) _92²³⁵ U can undergo fission readily.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q5
rows.question: Explain what causes the wind to blow in equatorial regions. What is wind energy?
rows.answer: Solar energy is responsible for wind to blow. The intensity of sun-rays is much more stronger near the equator of the earth than in the polar region. Due to more intense heat, the air near the surface of the earth in equatorial regions become quite hot. The hot air being lighter rises upward and cooler air from the polar region of earth starts flowing which causes wind to blow from the high pressure to low pressure region. So wind blows to equatorial regions. The energy possessed by this wind is called wind energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q6
rows.question: What is biogas? How can biogas be obtained? Why is the use of biogas obtained from cow dung advised in preference to burning of cow dung cakes?
rows.answer:
Biogas. The gas produced by the decay of biomass in the presence of water by anaerobic micro-organisms in the absence of oxygen is called biogas. Biogas can be obtained by using fixed dome type biogas plant.
The burning of cow-dung cakes is not advisable because of the following reasons :
(i) It produces a lot of ash as residue.
(ii) It produces a lot of smoke causes air pollution as well as creates health hazards.
(iii) It has low heat efficient fuel.
(iv) It destroys the useful manure.
Whereas during the production of biogas only organic matter is decomposed by anaerobic bacteria and convert into biogas. So use of biogas obtained from cow dung advised in preference gives (i) a clean fuel, free from pollution and (ii) spent slurry can be used as a manure.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q7
rows.question: Name three forms in which energy from ocean is made available for use. What are OTEC power plants ? How do they operate?
rows.answer:
Three forms of oceanic energy are
(a) Sea wave energy
(b) Tidal energy
(c) Ocean thermal energy.
OTEC power plant. The plants which are used to harness ocean thermal energy is called OTEC power plant.
Working of OTEC power plant. A temperature difference between warm surface water heated by sun and colder water at deeper level upto 1000 m is 20° C or more is required to operate OTEC plant.
In the OTEC system, the warm surface water is used to boil a liquid like ammonia. The vapour of liquid is then used to rotate the turbine of a generator. The cold water from the deeper level is used to convert the ammonia vapour again into liquid.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q8
rows.question: Describe the construction of solar cooker. How does it cause rise in temperature to cook food?
rows.answer:
Construction. It consists of an insulated metal box which is painted all black from inside. There is thick glass sheet as a cover over the box and a plane mirror reflector attached to the box.
Cooking of food. The food to be cooked is placed in a metal container kept in the box and covered with glass sheet. When solar energy falls on reflector, the reflector sends them to the top of solar cooker in the form of strong beam of sunlight which is absorbed by the black surface in the box. The infra-red rays cause heating effect which raises the temperature to 100° C − 140° C which cooks the food.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Long Answer Questions — Q9
rows.question:
(i) Distinguish between renewable and non-renewable sources of energy giving one example of each.
(ii) Why is the use of wood as a fuel not advised although forests can be replenished?
rows.answer:
(i) Renewable sources of energy are those that can be used without depleting their reserves and do not get exhausted e.g., solar energy, wind energy, hydro energy, etc.
Non-renewable sources of energy are those which cannot be replenished and exhausted with the passage of time. e.g., : coal, pertroleum and natural gas.
(ii) Wood is not advisable used as a fuel, although forests can be replenished, due to the following reasons :
(a) Burning of wood causes pollution.
(b) The left-over residue after combustion have a disposed off problem.
(c) Smoke produced by burning of wood causes health hazard.
(d) Wood is a less heat generating fuel.
(e) Trees are essential for our life as they absorb C O2 and give out O2.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Reasoning Based Questions — Q1
rows.question: Coal is said to be formed from the wood of trees. Why then is coal considered to be a non-renewable source of energy whereas wood is a renewable source of energy ?
rows.answer:
Wood is a renewable source of energy because if trees are cut from the forests for obtaining wood, then more trees will grow in the forest in due course of time.
Coal is a non-renewable source of energy because coal has accumulated in the earth over a very, very long time and if all the coal gets exhausted, it cannot be produced quickly in nature.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Reasoning Based Questions — Q2
rows.question: The energy in petrol originally came from the sun. Explain how it get into petrol.
rows.answer: Petrol is a fossi fuel which is produced from the remains of plants and animals that were buried in the earth long ago. The green plants need sunlight energy to grow. They get this energy from sunlight and store in the form of carbon compounds. The animals also eat plants, so the animal material is also a store house of sun's energy thus we can say, the energy in pertol originally came from the sun.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Reasoning Based Questions — Q3
rows.question: Why is the copper tube of a solar water heater painted black from outside?
rows.answer: Because black painted surface absorbs more heat rays of the sun.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Reasoning Based Questions — Q4
rows.question: Which of the two is a cleaner fuel : Hydrogen or CNG ? Why?
rows.answer: Hydrogen is a cleaner fuel than CNG. This is because burning of hydrogen produces only water which is not harmful. On the other hand, burning of CNG produces carbon dioxide gas and water. This carbon dioxide can produce greenhouse effect in the atmosphere and lead to the excessive heating of the environment.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q1
rows.question: "A burning match stick can ignite a piece of paper or an incense stick but cannot burn a wooden block". Given reasons.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q2
rows.question: Water that falls from a waterfall is used to generate electricity. If the height of water fall is 100 m and 10² m³ of water falls every minute, then find the hydel energy in MW h generated in one day. Assume that 60 % of the energy of the flowing water is converted into electricity. (Take density of water as 1000 k g m−³ ).
rows.answer:
Let the volume of water that flows in every minute = V.
Density of water (d) = m/V
One day = 24 × 60 minutes
Mass of the water that flows in one day is m= V d × (60 × 24)
Potential energy, PE = m g h
The energy of the flowing water in one day is = m g h 60 % of the ( mgh ) is used to generate electricity
∴ Energy generated = 24 M W h

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q3
rows.question: Among methane (C H4) and methyl alcohol (C H3 O H) which is a better fuel and why?
rows.answer: C H4 is a better fuel.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q4
rows.question: Find the electrical power generated in 1 s by a wind mill that is erected in a location when air flows with an average speed of 54 k m h−¹. Assume that the moving air is completely stopped by the blades of the wind turbine of area 10 m², over which air flows normally and the energy conversion from wind energy to electrical energy is only 50 %. (Take density of air = 1.3 k g m−³ )
rows.answer:
The volume of air that makes blades to rotate = V= ℓ × area of the blade
Velocity (v) = ℓ/t
Mass of the air = m= volume × density of air
Kinetic energy (KE) of air = 1/2 m v²
50 % of kinetic energy of air is converted into electrical energy (EE)
The power of electrical generation is, P= (E E)/(1 s) = 2.53 k W

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q7
rows.question:
Explain why :
(i) It is difficult to burn a piece of wood fresh from a tree.
(ii) Pouring dry sand over the fire extinguishes it.
(iii) It is difficult to use hydrogen as a source of energy.
(iv) Charcoal is considered a better fuel than wood.
rows.answer:
(i) It is because a piece of fresh wood is not dry and therefore it is to be heated at high temperature before it catches fire, that is why it is difficult to burn.
(ii) It cuts off the supply of air (oxygen) which is required for combustion to take place.
(iii) Hydrogen is highly combustible and burns with an explosion, therefore, it is difficult to store and transport.
(iv) Charcoal, has higher calorific value than wood and produces less smoke than wood.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q8
rows.question: Name the major fuel component of biogas. What are its other combustible components? What is the use for the residual slurry and why?
rows.answer:
Major fuel component of biogas-Methane. Other combustile component of biogas-Hydrogen, Hydrogen sulphide, Carbon dioxide.
Use of residue slurry. Left-over slurry from the biogas plant is used as a manure because it is rich in nitrogen and phosphorus compounds and can be used in place of nitrogeneous and phosphorus fertilizers.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions — Hots Questions — Q9
rows.question: What is the main basic cause for wind to blow ? Name a part of India where wind energy is commercially harnessed. Compare wind power and power of water flow in respect of generating mechanical and electrical energies. What is the hindrance in developing them ?
rows.answer:
Cause of wind to blow.
Unequal heating of the different land mass and water bodies such as river, lake, ocean etc. by solar radiations creates a different pressure regions. The flow of air from higher pressure region i.e., polar region towards the lower pressure region i.e., equatorial region, constitutes wind.
It is Kanya Kumari in Tamil Nadu where wind energy is commercially harnessed.
Wind power potential. From the wind power India generates only 2000 MW whereas India has the potential to generate 45000 MW of electrical power from wind energy.
Hydro power potential. It is estimated that India can generate about 4 × 10¹¹ k W h of electricity when its hydro power potential is fully exploited. At present, India can exploit only 11 % of its potential capacity.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q1
rows.question: What is a good source of energy ?
rows.answer:
A good source of energy has the following characteristics:
(i) It could do a large amount of work per unit volume of mass.
(ii) It should be easily accessible.
(iii) It should be easy to store and transport.
(iv) It should be economical.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q2
rows.question: What is a good fuel ?
rows.answer:
(a) It should have high calorific value.
(b) It should have low ignition temperature.
(c) It should not leave more ashes.
(d) It should not produce harmful gases on burning.
(e) It should be economic and easy to handle.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q3
rows.question: If you could use any source of energy for heating your food, which one would you use and why?
rows.answer:
If we are in a rural area, gobar-gas or biogas should be used as they are easily available there and are almost pollution free. Also, they have high calorific value.
In a city, LPG should be used since it doesn't produce pollution, it is easily available and economical than other sources.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q4
rows.question: What are the disadvantages of fossil fuels ?
rows.answer:
(i) They are formed under the earth's surface by a long process which takes million of years to form.
(ii) It is non-renewable source of energy.
(iii) They cause air pollution

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q5
rows.question: Why are we looking at alternate sources of energy?
rows.answer: Because of our changing life style and new technology we want more efficient fuels which are pollution free, cheap and renewable which cannot be fulfilled by using traditional sources of energy.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q6
rows.question: How has the traditional use of wind \& water energy been modified for our convenience?
rows.answer: Earlier, the wind mills were used to harnesswind energy to do mechanical work such as lifting/drawing water from well. Today, windmills are used to generate electricity. Traditionally, water falls were used as a source of potential energy which was converted into electricity with the help of turbines. Today, hydrodams are used to harness potential energy.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q7
rows.question: What kind of mirror-concave, convex or plane- would be best suited for use in a solar cooker? Why?
rows.answer: Plane mirror is best suited for a solar cooker because it reflects the parallel rays of sunlight to the top of solar cooker box in the form of a strong beam of sunlight, which helps the food to cook fast.

rows.title: Exercise 2 Master NCERT (Textbook \& Exemplar) — Text-Book Questions — Q8
rows.question: What are the limitations of the energy that can be obtained from the oceans?
rows.answer:
The energy from the ocean can be obtained mainly in three forms- (i) tidal energy (ii) wave energy and ocean thermal energy. But these energy can't be a potential source of energy in future because of the following reasons -
(i) there are very few places around the world which are suitable for building tidal dams.

rows.question: In a nuclear reactor U²³⁵ undergoes fission liberating 200 MeV of energy. The reactor has 10 % efficiency and
rows.answer:
( 3.8 × 10⁴ k g ) If m k g is the required mass of the uranium, then number of nuclei
= ((m × 1000) × 6.02 × 10²³)/(235)
Each U U²³⁵ nucleus releases energy 200 MeV ,
∴ total energy released in 10 years
Ei n= (m × 6.02 × 10²⁶)/(235) × 200
Energy required in 10 years, Eour= P t
= (1000 × 10⁶) × (10 × 365 × 24 × 3600)
Efficiency η= Eout/Ein
Substituting the values, we get
m= 3.8 × 10⁴ k g
